sglang.title: [Fix] Serialize FanOutCommunicator queueing calls with a FIFO-fair asyncio.Lock
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Concurrent /server_info requests can intermittently return 500: FanOutCommunicator.queueing_call has a wakeup-window race where a caller arriving between the previous caller's cleanup and the queued waiter's wakeup bypasses the ready queue and trips assert self._result_event is None (seen in schedul

sglang.title: Disable multi-threaded load by default when prefetch is on
sglang.author: mmangkad
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
#20289 turned enable_multithread_load on by default. So when you also enable --weight-loader-prefetch-checkpoints, you get two things reading the same shards at the same time, the prefetch threads warming the page cache and the multi-threaded loader pulling the same files. On NFS/Lustre that

sglang.title: [PDD] Add true request retraction for PDD
sglang.author: MrAta
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
More context here in sglang-miles branch: scheduler.py:L3756-L3763
Currently we can't do true request retraction in PDD mode. This PR adds it: on retract, the decode worker drops the KV cache and, on  continue_generation , asks the  original prefill worker to recompute the prefix KV under

sglang.title: [Fix] Prevent silent VLM server crash when /dev/shm is exhausted during multimodal feature transport
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Under a burst of multimodal requests, ShmPointerMMData can exhaust /dev/shm mid-copy and kill the server with SIGBUS (tmpfs pages are allocated lazily after ftruncate). Reserve pages with posix_fallocate so exhaustion raises a catchable OSError, and fall back to inline (pickled) transport instead of

sglang.title: [AMD] Enable JIT staged HiCache write-back and fix CPU-index crash
sglang.author: AMD-yanfeiwang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
On ROCm, page_first + kernel HiCache write-back crashes on the first prefill with RuntimeError: Destination indices must be a CUDA tensor. This PR fixes the underlying cause so ROCm runs the same page_first + kernel JIT staged write-back path as CUDA, instead of the #28473 layer_first fallback.
Root

sglang.title: [AMD] Fix AITER custom all-gather CUDA-graph capture crash under torch_memory_saver
sglang.author: JessicaJiang-123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Co-authored-with: @XinyuJiangCMU

Motivation

On ROCm, the AITER custom all-gather calls all_gather_reg during CUDA-graph capture. When the CUDA-graph memory pool is managed by torch_memory_saver, that buffer is a HIP VMM allocation, and registering it via hipIpcGetMemHandle fails at capture end and

sglang.title: [Bench] Add fixed-prompt mode and per-request spec accept length metrics
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Bench/eval tooling for speculative decoding: --fixed-prompt-file + --apply-chat-template in one_batch_server for a controlled constant accept length across batch sizes, and per-request spec_accept_length collection in bench_serving and run_eval.

sglang.title: [refactor] Move the EP dispatcher and fusion-workspace manager state onto ctx.resources
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The DeepEP, Mooncake, and NIXL dispatcher buffer managers each held their comm-buffer handle and sizing metadata (and DeepEP's dispatch-mode state machine) as class attributes, and the FlashInfer allreduce-fusion workspace kept two module-level manager instances (attn-TP / MoE-TP groups)

sglang.title: [refactor] Add the per-forward flags tier: ctx.forward
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Per-forward runtime state lived in per-module holders with inconsistent concurrency choices and lifecycles: a thread-local for the multi-stream switch, a bare contextvar for the MoE output buffer, per-forward slots mixed with init-resolved state on the AttnTpContext module singleton, and

sglang.title: [refactor] Split the DP gathered-buffer state between flags.dp and ctx.forward
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
_DpGatheredBufferWrapper held all of its state as bare class attributes, mixing two lifetimes: the allocation metadata (hidden size / dtype / device, resolved once at initialize_dp_attention) and the per-forward sizing quartet (global/local buffer lengths, max-padding mode, global token c

sglang.title: [refactor] Adopt get_parallel() everywhere and close out the parallel wrapper surface
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
runtime_context.ParallelContext is the read-through surface for parallel topology, but adoption had gaps: the decode-context-parallel dimension was never wrapped, a few files regressed back to raw getters inside the already-swept directories, dp_attention still exported a re-export family

sglang.title: Add opt-in SGLANG_ROPE_CACHE_FP32 to keep RoPE cache in fp32 on non-CUDA
sglang.author: zx3xyy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The RoPE cos/sin cache is kept in fp32 only on CUDA; every other backend
downcasts it to the model dtype:
# NOTE(ByronHsu): cache needs to be in FP32 for numerical stability
if not _is_cuda:
    cache = cache.to(dtype)
On non-CUDA backends (observed on ROCm) this loses precision relative

sglang.title: [refactor] Retire the legacy config accessor and the remaining process singletons
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The config tier has one blessed accessor (runtime_context.get_server_args) but 280 call sites still went through the legacy get_global_server_args name; a few process singletons (the indexer/routed-experts state capturers, the shared TCPStore, the trace verbosity level) still lived as mod

sglang.title: [docs] Add the sglang-runtime-context skill

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The runtime-context architecture now spans several tiers and CI guardrails; contributors (and coding agents) need one onboarding reference for how to read and mutate configuration, declare model-specific adjustments, and test against it.
Modifications
Adds the sglang-runtime-context skill

sglang.title: [AMD] fix dsv4 indexer dtype dispatch on gfx950
sglang.author: billishyahao
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The DeepSeek-V4 indexer and the unified-KV paged-decode kernel selected their FP8 data type with is_hip(), which hardcodes torch.float8_e4m3fnuz for all AMD GPUs. This is only correct on CDNA3 (gfx942).  This PR aligns the dtype so the dispatch is correct on gfx950 (and unchanged on gfx94

sglang.title: [Feat][GLM5.2] Add DSA Cache Layer Split under Prefill CP
sglang.author: Dovis01
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Support new features for GLM5.2 Model. Add DSA cache layer-split for DeepSeek Sparse Attention under prefill context-parallel: the KV/indexer cache layers are sharded across CP ranks (each rank owns a disjoint layer range) instead of every rank holding all layers, cutting per-rank KV memory

sglang.title: Support per-regex diff-threshold predicates in the tensor comparator
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Replace the comparator's single global float --diff-threshold with per-tensor
pass criteria: a list of (regex, predicate) rules where a tensor is judged by
the first rule whose regex fullmatches its name.
Motivation: RL training dumps contain near-zero tensors (e.g. grads of starved
MoE experts) who

sglang.title: Fix rel_diff being nan for bitwise-identical tensors
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
calc_rel_diff on two bitwise-identical tensors evaluates 0/0 and returns NaN.
Any comparison against NaN is False, so under the predicate DSL a strict
'rel <= 0' criterion (exact-match checking, e.g. deterministic-mode dumps)
spuriously fails precisely on the tensors that match perfectly - including

sglang.title: Cap diagnostic detail computation for failing tensors
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Percentile computation dominates per-tensor comparator cost (a
numpy.percentile call is roughly 10-40x the rel_diff reduction) while being
purely diagnostic: percentiles and samples feed the formatter output and
never affect the pass/fail verdict. When most tensors fail (e.g. a
systematic divergence

sglang.title: Support grad injection and step override in the dumper's model dump
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Two additions for dumping training-side (Megatron) model params so they can be
compared against a baseline:

dump_model / _dump_inner accept an optional get_grad callable.
Distributed-optimizer setups do not populate param.grad; the caller knows
where the real grad lives (e.g. main_grad buckets) and

sglang.title: [GDN] Auto-select FlashInfer GDN prefill on validated SM100 configs
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
SGLang ships the FlashInfer GDN prefill kernel, but GDN prefill still defaults to Triton when no per-phase backend is specified.
On B200, FlashInfer 0.6.12 provides repeatable prefill gains when static chunking is limited to 8192 tokens. This PR enables it by default only for that validat

sglang.title: [AMD] Fix int32 offset overflow in Triton decode-attention kernels
sglang.author: yuankaichen-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt: The decode-attention stage1/stage1-grouped/stage2 kernels index the flat Mid_O/Mid_O_1 scratch buffers using cur_batch * stride, where cur_batch = tl.program_id(0) is int32. When max_kv_splits is large (e.g. ceil(context_len/256) ~ 792 for long-context MLA models) and CUDA graph captures large batch

sglang.title: [Bugfix] Migrate retired parallel accessors
sglang.author: mmangkad
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Main CI broke from a merge-order mismatch between a few recent PRs.
#30492 and #30493 moved the codebase over to get_parallel() and retired the old parallel helper calls. #29421 added the GLM/DSA cache layer-split code while still using the old CP helpers, and #25381 added the GLM Image AR c

sglang.title: [AMD] Fix stale SWA ring buffer on radix prefix reuse for DeepSeek-V4 with unified_kv backend
sglang.author: amd-danli103
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

The DeepSeek-V4 unified_kv backend under ROCm stores SWA KV in a per-request ring buffer, addressed by req_pool_idx * window + pos % window. Unlike the compressed/global KV, this ring is not content-addressed and is never written into the radix tree.
Radix prefix caching reuses matched p

sglang.title: fix(mtp): avoid mtp perf regression in deepseek when enable eplb
sglang.author: ICENacl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When EPLB and MTP are enabled in DeepSeek, the draft model reusing the target model's dispatch info causes a degradation in accept rate. We introduce the is_nextn flag to fix this issue. This approach is consistent with the implementation in Qwen3 MoE (see _forward_deepep in Qwen2MoeSpars

sglang.title: Fix garbage output for bare-tekken Mistral checkpoints (e.g. Leanstral)
sglang.author: JustinTong0323

Motivation
Serving mistralai/Leanstral-1.5-119B-A6B (and any Mistral-native checkpoint that ships only tekken.json, no tokenizer.json) produces pure garbage output on every hardware / attention backend / TP config, while the same weights work fine on vLLM.
Root cause is in the tokenizer, not the mod

sglang.title: [AMD] Enable unified-KV HiCache on DeepSeek-V4
sglang.author: 1am9trash
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

On DeepSeek-V4, HiCache already works with the separate KV layout used by the standalone attention backends (e.g. triton, tilelang). However, the unified_kv_triton backend uses a different KV layout — SWA ring, C4, and C128 packed into a single unified pool — which is incompatible with t

Motivation
BCG captures layer_model.forward directly, while PCG goes through the outer Qwen3.5 multimodal wrapper. That outer wrapper replaces rank-1 positions with rank-2 forward_batch.mrope_positions when MRoPE is enabled. BCG bypassed that substitution, so MRotaryEmbedding.forward_cuda did not ta

sglang.bodyExcerpt:

e8m0_to_f32 in python/sglang/srt/layers/quantization/quark/utils.py decodes
OCP MX-format v1.0 e8m0 (uint8 unsigned exponent, no mantissa) into float32
scales. Per spec, encoded 0..254 represents 2^(x − 127) and encoded 255
is reserved to represent NaN.
The existing predicate compared on

sglang.title: [Quantization][Bugfix]: Join multi-arg RuntimeError in Quark _check_scheme_supported

QuarkConfig._check_scheme_supported raises a capability-mismatch error like this:
raise RuntimeError(
    "Quantization scheme is not supported for ",
    f"the current GPU. Min capability: {min_capability}. ",
    f"Current capability: {capability}.",
)
That passes three positional argum

sglang.title: Make KvVmmArena JIT stub unique per process
sglang.author: cctry
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
The KvVmmArena JIT stub .so is built in a host-shared tempdir. With only a per-instance suffix, co-located engine processes build the same-named .so and race on the shared build dir/main.cpp — one process can compile another's source and load a .so missing its own symbols (undefined-symbol c

sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Continuation of the admission-criteria pruning from #30690, covering 23 unit test files not in the first pass. Every deletion is backed by a stronger kept case or a verified dead-code/dead-patch claim; each was cross-checked by a verification agent.
Also sharpens .claude/rules/unit-test-admission.md

sglang.title: Fix pipeline-parallel abort missing in-flight requests in non-current microbatch slots
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
TODO: add co-author of those 2 prs when merging
Problem
abort_request's "abort already-running requests" path only scanned self.running_batch plus a stale self.cur_batch. Under pipeline parallelism the scheduler keeps per-microbatch-slot state in running_mbs[] / mbs[], and at abort time self.running

sglang.title: Stop reading cur_batch in is_fully_idle and abort_request
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This PR removes every load-bearing read of self.cur_batch outside the PP microbatch slot arrays, in three steps. Two are behavior-preserving; one is an intentional, disclosed narrowing.
The enabling invariant it establishes: abort_request only ever runs at the top of the event loop, where self.cur_b

sglang.title: Localize cur_batch field in Scheduler to avoid field-based state access
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: self.cur_batch was a scheduler-wide attribute written by every event loop (normal, overlap, PP, disagg prefill/decode, MLX) but its only cross-thread reader is the watchdog, which uses it purely as a debugging signal (is_active / dump_info). All in-loop consumers can read the batch from a local inst

sglang.title: Configurable decode retraction order
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When the KV cache fills during decode, requests are retracted to free memory. The existing policy always retracts short-output, long-input requests first, which is not always desirable — some deployments want to retract lower-priority work first.
Changes

Add a --retraction-policy server

sglang.title: Avoid implicit field-based side channel in Scheduler planning
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: get_next_batch_to_run and its callee tree (plus the disaggregation analogues get_next_disagg_prefill_batch_to_run / get_next_disagg_decode_batch_to_run and their trees) used self.cur_batch / self.last_batch / self.running_batch as an implicit in/out side channel. This PR makes those reads and writes

vllm.title: [Bugfix][MRV2] Reset num_accepted_tokens on add_request in all modes
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Under P/D disaggregation a decode-role request's prefill runs on the remote producer, so its first local step is a decode. The GDN/Mamba attention metadata reads num_accepted_tokens_gpu[req_index] before postprocess_state updates it, so without a reset the request inherits a stale value from the pre

Extensive testing of DeepSeekV3.2 with MTP on on

Sequence, that iteration performs a per-chunk __getitem__ — a memoryview
slice plus a BlockHash allocation — for every chunk, including the
spar

Fixes #47196
Summary
This patch orders ROCm AITER sparse MLA persistent metadata updates before graph replay consumes those metadata buffers.
The sparse MLA backend precomputes persistent metadata with get_mla_metadata_v1(). The resulting buffers are later passed to the AITER sparse MLA decode kerne

error: unexpected argument '--config' fo

Follow-up to #46544, implementing the direction confirmed there by @orozery: each tier is fully responsible for its own KV events, and "actual secondary tier event implementations should be on a follow-up".

The fs and obj secondary tiers each own an event buffer and override SecondaryTierMa

vllm.title: DCP supports hybrid attention

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add DCP support for hybrid-attention models. Hybrid-attention models such as Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B contain both DCP-capable full-attention layers and non-DCP layers. This PR enables DCP for supported
attention groups without globally blocking hybrid-attention DCP, while keeping
non-DCP groups on

vllm.title: [Core][KV events] Report prefix-cache-reused blocks in full report mode
vllm.author: GongLei-HW
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
vLLM's KV cache event publisher (enable_kv_cache_events) emits BlockStored from cache_full_blocks() — i.e. only for newly allocated blocks. Blocks reused from the prefix cache are never reported, because they are not freshly allocated. External consumers that build a global prefix tree fo
